title: Sabulina jacutica
taxonomy: Sabulina jacutica belongs to the family Aizoaceae and is classified as a succulent species. It is a type of plant that is native to the regions of Siberia and Kazakhstan. The genus Sabulina is a part of the subfamily Ruschioideae. Sabulina jacutica is often found growing in sandy or rocky areas. The exact classification of Sabulina jacutica may vary depending on the source.

intro: Sabulina jacutica, also known as Siberian Sabulina, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae, native to Siberia and the Russian Far East. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Sabulina jacutica include iridoid glycosides, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sabulina jacutica is commonly prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and fever.
